mcqs.question: What happens when a thread calls `fork()` in a multithreaded program?

options.text: Only the calling thread is duplicated

mcqs.explanation: Most systems duplicate only the calling thread to avoid unnecessary resource duplication.

mcqs.question: Which condition causes a deadlock?

options.text: Mutual exclusion, hold & wait, no preemption, circular wait

mcqs.explanation: Deadlocks require all four Coffman conditions to occur simultaneously.

mcqs.question: What is a race condition?

options.text: When threads access shared data inconsistently

mcqs.explanation: Race conditions arise from incorrect concurrent access to shared resources.

mcqs.question: How do mutexes prevent threading issues?
mcqs.explanation: Mutexes ensure only one thread can access critical sections at a time.

mcqs.question: What is priority inversion?

options.text: Higher-priority threads waiting for lower-priority ones

mcqs.explanation: Priority inversion occurs when a higher-priority thread is delayed by a lower-priority thread holding a resource.
